phases.name: JSON Decoding
phases.description: Implement the Decoder layer: NodeKind enum, ContainerNode struct, InkDecoder recursive JSON parser, and InkDecoder.probe() wired into Story.init(json:). Walking skeleton must be green by end of step 03.

steps.name: Implement NodeKind enum with all node types
steps.description: Replace the scaffold NodeKind enum with a complete, compilable enum covering every node type found in the spike: text, newline, intValue, floatValue, controlCommand, nativeFunction, divert, choicePoint, variableAssignment, variableReference, tagOpen, tagClose, voidValue, and container. All cases must be internal (Rule R2). No fatalErrors remain in this file.
criteria: NodeKind is declared `internal enum` — no `public` modifier anywhere on the type or its cases

All node kinds identified in spike/findings.md are present: .text, .newline, .intValue, .floatValue, .controlCommand, .nativeFunction, .divert, .choicePoint, .variableAssignment, .variableReference, .tagOpen, .tagClose, .voidValue, .container
`swift build` succeeds with zero errors and zero warnings on the SwiftInkRuntime target
No import of Engine or Facade layer types in NodeKind.swift (Rule R1)
steps.notes: No acceptance test is enabled for this step — correctness is verified by the decoder tests in step 01-02 and 01-04. Build-green is the gate.

steps.name: Implement ContainerNode and InkDecoder.decode(_:) recursive parser
steps.description: Implement the recursive JSON-to-ContainerNode parser in InkDecoder using JSONSerialization. Apply the container invariant from the spike: last element null means no metadata; last element dict carries #f (flags), #n (name), and named sub-containers. Integer and float values must be correctly distinguished. ContainerNode struct must be fully populated from real JSON. Enable and green the first two Milestone 1 tests.
criteria: Enabling `decoder parses minimal Ink JSON into a ContainerNode tree` and running `swift test --filter Milestone1_JSONDecodingTests/decoder_parses_minimal_Ink_JSON_into_a_ContainerNode_tree` produces a green result
Enabling `container null last element produces empty named content and zero flags` and running `swift test` produces a green result
Enabling `container dict last element populates named sub-containers` and running `swift test` produces a green result
Enabling `decoder classifies whole numbers as intValue not floatValue` and running `swift test` produces a green result
JSONSerialization is only called from InkDecoder.swift — no call sites in Engine or Facade (Rule R3)
`swift build` succeeds with zero errors
steps.scenario_name: `decoder parses minimal Ink JSON into a ContainerNode tree`

steps.name: Implement InkDecoder.probe() and wire into Story.init(json:)
steps.description: Implement InkDecoder.probe() so it decodes the bundled test.ink.json fixture, asserts the result is a ContainerNode with at least one child, and verifies JSONSerialization can round-trip the fixture. Wire Story.init(json:) to call probe() before constructing the engine — throw StoryError.decoderProbeFailure(reason:) if probe fails, StoryError.invalidJSON for malformed input, and StoryError.unsupportedInkVersion for unsupported inkVersion values. This step must green the Walking Skeleton acceptance tests.
criteria: Running `swift test --filter WalkingSkeletonTests` produces green for both: `story loads from a real bundle fixture and canContinue is true` and `Story init throws for malformed JSON`
Enabling `InkDecoder probe passes for the bundled test fixture` and running `swift test` produces a green result
Enabling `Story init throws for an unsupported ink version` and running `swift test` produces a green result
Story.init(json:) throws StoryError.decoderProbeFailure when probe() fails
Story.init(json:) throws StoryError.invalidJSON for non-JSON input
`swift build` succeeds with zero errors
steps.scenario_name: `story loads from a real bundle fixture and canContinue is true`

steps.name: Enable and green all Milestone 1 acceptance tests
steps.description: Remove all .disabled(...) attributes from Milestone1_JSONDecodingTests.swift. All six tests must pass. Fix any remaining decoder issues discovered by the full suite. This step is purely test-enabling — if 01-01 through 01-03 are correct, no new production code should be required.
criteria: Running `swift test --filter Milestone1_JSONDecodingTests` produces green for all six tests: `decoder parses minimal Ink JSON into a ContainerNode tree`, `InkDecoder probe passes for the bundled test fixture`, `Story init throws for an unsupported ink version`, `container null last element produces empty named content and zero flags`, `container dict last element populates named sub-containers`, `decoder classifies whole numbers as intValue not floatValue`
Running `swift test --filter WalkingSkeletonTests` remains green for both walking skeleton tests
No test is annotated with .disabled in the Milestone 1 test file
`swift test` exits with code 0 — zero failures, zero errors
steps.scenario_name: `decoder parses minimal Ink JSON into a ContainerNode tree`

phases.name: Story Execution
phases.description: Implement the Engine and Facade layers: TagParser pure functions, StoryState struct, TreeWalker recursive visitor, InkEngine orchestrator, and the Story facade wiring all layers together. The oracle comparison test drives correctness — output must match InkStory line-by-line.

steps.name: Implement TagParser pure functions
steps.description: Implement TagParser.parse(_:) to convert a raw Ink tag string into a (key, value) tuple. A tag of the form 'key: value' returns key and optional value. A bare tag returns the full string as key with nil value. This is a pure function with no side effects — no JSONSerialization, no imports beyond Swift standard library.
criteria: TagParser.parse("IMAGE: background.png") returns (key: "IMAGE", value: "background.png")
TagParser.parse("NOTE") returns (key: "NOTE", value: nil)
TagParser.parse("key: value with colon: inside") treats only the first colon as the separator
TagParser.parse("") returns (key: "", value: nil) without crashing
No import of Foundation, Decoder, or Facade layer types in TagParser.swift (Rule R1)
`swift build` succeeds with zero errors
steps.notes: TagParser is a pure domain function — its public signature IS the driving port. Unit tests for TagParser call it directly. No acceptance test is enabled in this step; correctness is verified transitively when tags appear in Milestone 2 tests.

steps.name: Implement StoryState struct with all fields for tree-walker execution
steps.description: Define all stored properties on StoryState required by the tree-walker: current pointer (container reference + index), call stack for knot/stitch navigation, visit counts dictionary, output stream buffer, variables state dictionary, current tags list, and a flag indicating whether the story has ended. StoryState must remain Codable (already declared in scaffold). No fatalErrors remain.
criteria: StoryState compiles as a struct with Codable conformance — `swift build` succeeds

StoryState contains at minimum: a pointer to the current position in the container tree, an output stream buffer, a variables state store, a visit counts store, a current tags collection, and an isEnded flag
All stored properties use Codable-compatible types (no raw ContainerNode references stored directly — use path-based addressing)
No import of Facade layer types in StoryState.swift (Rule R1)
`swift build` succeeds with zero errors
steps.notes: StoryState fields are driven by what TreeWalker and InkEngine need. Define the minimal set required to green Milestone 2 tests — do not add fields speculatively.

steps.name: Implement TreeWalker — recursive visitor dispatching on NodeKind
steps.description: Implement TreeWalker.step(in:state:) to advance execution by one node, updating StoryState in place. Dispatch on NodeKind: text nodes append to output stream; newline nodes flush line; control commands (ev, /ev, str, /str, pop, done, end) update execution mode; diverts update the current pointer; choice points register available choices; variable assignments update variables state; variable references read from variables state; tagOpen/tagClose accumulate current tags. The walker must handle the recursive container structure — named sub-containers are reachable via divert targets.
criteria: TreeWalker.step(in:state:) returns nil when the container is exhausted (no more nodes at current index)
Text nodes are accumulated in StoryState output stream
Newline nodes cause a line boundary in the output stream
A done or end control command sets StoryState.isEnded to true
No calls to JSONSerialization in TreeWalker.swift (Rule R3)
`swift build` succeeds with zero errors
steps.notes: TreeWalker is the most complex component. Implement incrementally, driven by unit tests for each NodeKind case. Use InkEngine (step 02-04) as the orchestration harness during integration.

steps.name: Implement InkEngine — owns state, drives walker, exposes computed properties
steps.description: Implement InkEngine with a complete init(root:) and orchestrate the TreeWalker to advance story execution, producing paragraphs of output. Expose all computed properties: canContinue, currentText, currentChoices, currentTags, globalTags, currentErrors. Implement chooseChoice(at:) to validate the index and update the current position. Implement saveState() and restoreState(_:) via state serialization on StoryState. No fatalErrors remain.
criteria: InkEngine.init(root:) initialises StoryState to the beginning of the root container without crashing
InkEngine.canContinue returns true when there are unvisited nodes and the story has not ended
InkEngine.step() advances the walker until a line of output is ready or canContinue becomes false
InkEngine.currentText returns the most recently completed output line
InkEngine.currentChoices returns the choices registered by the walker at the current choice point
InkEngine.chooseChoice(at:) throws StoryError.invalidChoiceIndex for an out-of-range index
InkEngine.saveState() returns Data encodable from StoryState via JSONEncoder
InkEngine.restoreState(_:) decodes Data via JSONDecoder into StoryState; throws StoryError.invalidStateData for undecodable input
`swift build` succeeds with zero errors

steps.name: Wire Story facade and green all Milestone 2 tests
steps.description: Replace all fatalError stubs in Story.swift with delegation to InkEngine. Story.init(json:) calls InkDecoder.probe(), decodes the JSON into ContainerNode, and initialises InkEngine. All public properties and methods delegate to the engine. Remove all .disabled(...) attributes from Milestone2_StoryExecutionTests.swift. All seven tests must pass, including the oracle comparison against InkStory.
criteria: Running `swift test --filter Milestone2_StoryExecutionTests` produces green for all seven tests: `linear story can be continued through all text without error`, `SwiftInkRuntime output matches InkSwift oracle for the test fixture`, `choices appear in currentChoices after continuing a passage`, `selecting a choice advances the story to the chosen path`, `chooseChoice throws for an out-of-range index`, `tags from a tagged passage appear in currentTags after continue`, `story ends gracefully with no errors when fully continued`
Running `swift test --filter WalkingSkeletonTests` remains green
Running `swift test --filter Milestone1_JSONDecodingTests` remains green
No test is annotated with .disabled in the Milestone 2 test file
No import of InkSwift in any production source file under Sources/SwiftInkRuntime/ (Rule from Decision 2a)
`swift test` exits with code 0
steps.scenario_name: `SwiftInkRuntime output matches InkSwift oracle for the test fixture`

phases.name: Save and Restore
phases.description: Implement the save/restore cycle using the Codable StoryState. InkEngine owns saveState() and restoreState(_:); Story delegates to the engine. All four Milestone 3 acceptance tests must be green.

steps.name: Verify StoryState Codable conformance encodes and decodes a mid-story snapshot
steps.description: Confirm that StoryState (declared Codable in Phase 02) can round-trip through JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder with all fields populated at a mid-story point. If any fields added in Phase 02 are not Codable-compatible, fix them now. This step may require no new production code if Phase 02 already produced a complete Codable StoryState.
criteria: A StoryState populated by stepping an InkEngine through at least one passage of test.ink.json can be encoded to Data by JSONEncoder without throwing
The encoded Data can be decoded back to a StoryState by JSONDecoder without throwing
The decoded StoryState equals the original StoryState (same current pointer, same output history, same visit counts)
`swift build` succeeds with zero errors
steps.notes: If StoryState.Codable is already complete from Phase 02, this step is a verification-only gate with no production file changes.

steps.name: Implement InkEngine.saveState() and InkEngine.restoreState(_:)
steps.description: Implement InkEngine.saveState() to encode the current StoryState to Data using JSONEncoder. Implement InkEngine.restoreState(_:) to decode Data into StoryState using JSONDecoder, replacing the current state; throw StoryError.invalidStateData if decoding fails. Both methods are already scaffolded — remove fatalErrors and provide real implementations.
criteria: InkEngine.saveState() returns non-empty Data after at least one step has been taken
InkEngine.restoreState(_:) applied to Data from saveState() produces a StoryState equal to the saved snapshot
InkEngine.restoreState(_:) throws StoryError.invalidStateData when called with arbitrary non-JSON bytes
InkEngine.restoreState(_:) throws StoryError.invalidStateData when called with valid JSON that does not decode to StoryState
`swift build` succeeds with zero errors

steps.name: Wire Story save/restore and green all Milestone 3 tests
steps.description: Wire Story.saveState() and Story.restoreState(_:) to delegate to InkEngine. Remove all .disabled(...) attributes from Milestone3_SaveRestoreTests.swift. All four tests must pass: position preservation, choice-point restoration, invalid-data error, and round-trip stability.
criteria: Running `swift test --filter Milestone3_SaveRestoreTests` produces green for all four tests: `save and restore preserves the story position`, `restore places the story at the saved choice point`, `restoring with invalid data throws StoryError invalidStateData`, `save-restore round trip is stable across repeated saves`
Running `swift test --filter WalkingSkeletonTests` remains green
Running `swift test --filter Milestone1_JSONDecodingTests` remains green
Running `swift test --filter Milestone2_StoryExecutionTests` remains green
No test is annotated with .disabled in the Milestone 3 test file
`swift test` exits with code 0 — all 13 tests across all milestones and the walking skeleton pass
steps.scenario_name: `save and restore preserves the story position`

validation.review_notes: APPROVED after revision. Prescriptive HOW details removed from 01-02 and 02-04 descriptions. All DISTILL scenarios covered, walking skeleton in Phase 1, all step IDs valid, dependency chain valid.
